title: Living in Chesterfield, MO — Complete Guide
content:
Chesterfield is 25 miles west of downtown STL with ~50,000 residents. One of the largest St. Louis suburbs. ~30 min average commute.

Housing - Two Zip Codes:
63005 (Western/Newer): Larger modern/contemporary homes, brick exteriors, new construction. Median: ,000,000.
63017 (Eastern/Older): Brick ranch, traditional, split-level from 1970s-80s. Median: ,500.
Starters from K+. Condos/townhomes from K+.

History: Incorporated June 1, 1988, consolidating six towns. Named after Justus Post (War of 1812). Louis Sachs developed Chesterfield Village as pioneering live/work/play community. July 30, 1993 levee failure caused devastating flooding.

Shopping: Chesterfield Commons (longest outdoor strip mall in America). The District (The Hub outdoor venue, The Factory live music, breweries, restaurants). TopGolf.

New Developments: Chesterfield Sports Complex (9 basketball, 18 volleyball courts). Wildhorse Village (1M+ sqft office, 150+ homes, 500+ apartments, 100K sqft retail). Downtown Chesterfield (mall redevelopment, sustainability focus). Gateway Studios (32-acre production facility).

Schools: Rockwood School System. 15:1 student-teacher ratio. Lafayette and Marquette in MO's top 20%. State test scores exceed averages.

Economy: Growing healthcare, education, tech, finance sectors. Average hourly wages -. Median household income: ,052.

Parks: Faust Park (900 acres, Thornhill Mansion, Butterfly House, carousel). Central Park (55 acres, pool, amphitheater). Near Babler State Park.
